$comment: Tier-A registry config map: {registry_id -> {url, format, family, namespace?, ext_type, slug_column, notes}}. Decoupled from IANA's irregular download filenames; the closed `family` vocabulary is the permalink segment. Fetched + parsed deterministically by ingest/tier-a/run.mjs (no LLM in the loop). source_version is the upstream HTTP Last-Modified captured at fetch time.

http-status.notes: HTTP status codes. slug = numeric code. Skip range rows (e.g. 105-199) and Unassigned/Reserved.

http-method.notes: HTTP request methods. slug = method token (GET, PATCH, ...). All rows are assigned methods.

uri-scheme.notes: URI schemes. slug = scheme name. Curated to Status=Permanent (skip Provisional/Historical) for the Tier-A corpus.

dns-rrtype.notes: DNS resource record types. slug = mnemonic TYPE (AAAA). Skip Unassigned/Reserved/Private use rows.

cbor-tag.notes: CBOR tags. slug = tag number. Skip Unassigned and range rows (e.g. 6-15); single-integer tags only.

port.notes: Service names and port numbers. Per (service, transport) upstream; we collapse to one entry per port number, collecting transports. Curated to well-known system ports 0-1023 WITH a service name (skip the ~14000 user/dynamic and nameless rows).

tls-cipher.notes: TLS cipher suites. slug = concatenated hex value (0x00,0x01 -> 0x0001; TLS_AES_128_GCM_SHA256 0x13,0x01 -> 0x1301). Curated to Recommended=Y plus the classic TLS 1.3 suites and a set of common TLS 1.2 suites.
